---
title: "The European Social Survey, round 11 (2023/2024) - Trust"
format: 
  dashboard:
    theme:
      light: [litera, custom.scss]
      dark: [darkly, custom.scss]
    scrolling: true
    include-in-header:
      text: |
        <link rel="icon" href="favicon.svg" type="image/svg+xml">
        <link rel="preconnect" href="https://fonts.googleapis.com">
        <link href="https://fonts.googleapis.com/css2?family=Outfit:wght@400;500&family=Inter:wght@400;500&display=swap" rel="stylesheet">
    nav-buttons:
      - icon: envelope
        href: mailto:teromeh@proton.me
      - icon: house-door
        href: ../
---

```{r}
#| label: setup
#| include: false
library(plotly)
library(dplyr)
library(tidyr)
library(jsonlite)

#   agea     - age of respondent (numeric)
#   ppltrst  - most people can be trusted (0-10)
#   pplfair  - most people try to be fair (0-10)
#   pplhlp   - most people try to be helpful (0-10)
#   trstprl  - trust in country's parliament (0-10)
#   trstlgl  - trust in the legal system (0-10)
#   trstplc  - trust in the police (0-10)
#   trstplt  - trust in politicians (0-10)
#   trstprt  - trust in political parties (0-10)
#   trstep   - trust in the European Parliament (0-10)
#   trstun   - trust in the United Nations (0-10)

data <- readRDS("ess-11-trust-all.rds")

title_font <- list(family = "Outfit, sans-serif", size = 14, color = "#1a1a1a")

ess_red <- "#ED3D42"

# Darker shade of red to highlight each country's most common trust score.
darken_hex <- function(hex, factor = 0.8) {
  rgb_vals <- col2rgb(hex) * factor
  rgb(rgb_vals[1], rgb_vals[2], rgb_vals[3], maxColorValue = 255)
}
ess_red_dark <- darken_hex(ess_red, factor = 0.8)

country_names <- c(
  AT = "Austria", BE = "Belgium", BG = "Bulgaria", HR = "Croatia", CY = "Cyprus",
  CZ = "Czechia", EE = "Estonia", FI = "Finland", FR = "France", DE = "Germany",
  GR = "Greece", HU = "Hungary", IS = "Iceland", IE = "Ireland", IL = "Israel",
  IT = "Italy", LV = "Latvia", LT = "Lithuania", ME = "Montenegro",
  NL = "Netherlands", NO = "Norway", PL = "Poland", PT = "Portugal", RS = "Serbia",
  SK = "Slovakia", SI = "Slovenia", ES = "Spain", SE = "Sweden",
  CH = "Switzerland", UA = "Ukraine", GB = "United Kingdom"
)

cntry_codes <- sort(unique(data$cntry))
cntry_labels <- unname(ifelse(cntry_codes %in% names(country_names),
                               country_names[cntry_codes], cntry_codes))
name_order <- order(cntry_labels)
cntry_codes <- cntry_codes[name_order]
cntry_labels <- cntry_labels[name_order]

groups <- c("All", cntry_codes)
group_labels <- c("All", cntry_labels)

ppltrst_by_country <- lapply(groups, function(g) {
  d <- if (g == "All") data else data |> filter(cntry == g)
  d |>
    filter(!is.na(ppltrst)) |>
    count(ppltrst) |>
    mutate(percentage = n / sum(n) * 100,
           percentage_precise = sprintf("%.1f", percentage))
})

avg_scores <- vapply(ppltrst_by_country, function(d) {
  round(weighted.mean(d$ppltrst, w = d$n), 1)
}, numeric(1))

total_n <- vapply(ppltrst_by_country, function(d) sum(d$n), numeric(1))

bar_colors_by_country <- lapply(ppltrst_by_country, function(d) {
  ifelse(d$percentage == max(d$percentage), ess_red_dark, ess_red)
})

pplfair_by_country <- lapply(groups, function(g) {
  d <- if (g == "All") data else data |> filter(cntry == g)
  d |>
    filter(!is.na(pplfair)) |>
    count(pplfair) |>
    mutate(percentage = n / sum(n) * 100,
           percentage_precise = sprintf("%.1f", percentage))
})

avg_scores_fair <- vapply(pplfair_by_country, function(d) {
  round(weighted.mean(d$pplfair, w = d$n), 1)
}, numeric(1))

total_n_fair <- vapply(pplfair_by_country, function(d) sum(d$n), numeric(1))

bar_colors_by_country_fair <- lapply(pplfair_by_country, function(d) {
  ifelse(d$percentage == max(d$percentage), ess_red_dark, ess_red)
})

pplhlp_by_country <- lapply(groups, function(g) {
  d <- if (g == "All") data else data |> filter(cntry == g)
  d |>
    filter(!is.na(pplhlp)) |>
    count(pplhlp) |>
    mutate(percentage = n / sum(n) * 100,
           percentage_precise = sprintf("%.1f", percentage))
})

avg_scores_help <- vapply(pplhlp_by_country, function(d) {
  round(weighted.mean(d$pplhlp, w = d$n), 1)
}, numeric(1))

total_n_help <- vapply(pplhlp_by_country, function(d) sum(d$n), numeric(1))

bar_colors_by_country_help <- lapply(pplhlp_by_country, function(d) {
  ifelse(d$percentage == max(d$percentage), ess_red_dark, ess_red)
})

# Annotation helpers

avg_annotation <- function(avg) {
  list(
    text = paste0("Average trust level: <span style='color:#1a1a1a;font-weight:700;'>", avg, "</span>"),
    xref = "paper", x = 1, xanchor = "right",
    yref = "paper", y = 1.02, yanchor = "top",
    align = "right",
    showarrow = FALSE,
    font = list(family = "Inter, sans-serif", size = 12, color = "#555555")
  )
}

n_annotation <- function(n) {
  list(
    text = paste0("n: <span style='color:#1a1a1a;font-weight:700;'>",
                  formatC(n, big.mark = " ", format = "d"), "</span>"),
    xref = "paper", x = 1, xanchor = "right",
    yref = "paper", y = 0.96, yanchor = "top",
    align = "right",
    showarrow = FALSE,
    font = list(family = "Inter, sans-serif", size = 12, color = "#555555")
  )
}

avg_annotation_fair <- function(avg) {
  list(
    text = paste0("Average fairness level: <span style='color:#1a1a1a;font-weight:700;'>", avg, "</span>"),
    xref = "paper", x = 1, xanchor = "right",
    yref = "paper", y = 1.02, yanchor = "top",
    align = "right",
    showarrow = FALSE,
    font = list(family = "Inter, sans-serif", size = 12, color = "#555555")
  )
}

avg_annotation_help <- function(avg) {
  list(
    text = paste0("Average helpfulness level: <span style='color:#1a1a1a;font-weight:700;'>", avg, "</span>"),
    xref = "paper", x = 1, xanchor = "right",
    yref = "paper", y = 1.02, yanchor = "top",
    align = "right",
    showarrow = FALSE,
    font = list(family = "Inter, sans-serif", size = 12, color = "#555555")
  )
}

# Pre-compute the full per-country trace + annotation data

build_group_data <- function(by_country, avg_scores, total_n, bar_colors, avg_annotation_fn) {
  lapply(seq_along(groups), function(i) {
    d <- by_country[[i]]
    list(
      x = I(as.character(d[[1]])),
      y = I(d$percentage),
      text = I(paste0("<b>", d$percentage_precise, "%</b>")),
      hovertext = I(paste0("<b>", formatC(d$n, big.mark = " ", format = "d"), "</b> respondents")),
      marker_color = I(bar_colors[[i]]),
      annotations = list(avg_annotation_fn(avg_scores[i]), n_annotation(total_n[i]))
    )
  })
}

trust_group_data <- build_group_data(ppltrst_by_country, avg_scores, total_n,
                                      bar_colors_by_country, avg_annotation)
fair_group_data <- build_group_data(pplfair_by_country, avg_scores_fair, total_n_fair,
                                     bar_colors_by_country_fair, avg_annotation_fair)
help_group_data <- build_group_data(pplhlp_by_country, avg_scores_help, total_n_help,
                                     bar_colors_by_country_help, avg_annotation_help)

filter_js <- paste0(
  "window.essTrustData = ", as.character(jsonlite::toJSON(trust_group_data, auto_unbox = TRUE)), ";\n",
  "window.essFairData = ", as.character(jsonlite::toJSON(fair_group_data, auto_unbox = TRUE)), ";\n",
  "window.essHelpData = ", as.character(jsonlite::toJSON(help_group_data, auto_unbox = TRUE)), ";\n",
  "
function essFindGraphDiv(preferredId, positionIndex) {
  var el = document.getElementById(preferredId);
  if (el && el.data) return el;

  var all = Array.prototype.slice.call(document.querySelectorAll('.js-plotly-plot'));
  var byId = all.find(function (e) { return e.id && e.id.indexOf(preferredId) !== -1; });
  if (byId) return byId;

  if (all.length > positionIndex) return all[positionIndex];
  return null;
}

function essSetNavbarHeightVar() {
  var navbar = document.querySelector('.navbar');
  var h = navbar ? navbar.offsetHeight : 72;
  document.documentElement.style.setProperty('--ess-navbar-height', h + 'px');
}
essSetNavbarHeightVar();
window.addEventListener('resize', essSetNavbarHeightVar);
window.addEventListener('load', essSetNavbarHeightVar);

window.essUpdateCountry = function (idx) {
  try {
    var t = window.essTrustData[idx];
    var f = window.essFairData[idx];
    var h = window.essHelpData[idx];
    var trustEl = essFindGraphDiv('ppltrst-bar', 0);
    var fairEl = essFindGraphDiv('pplfair-bar', 1);
    var helpEl = essFindGraphDiv('pplhlp-bar', 2);
    console.log('[ess-filter] resolved elements:', trustEl ? trustEl.id : null, fairEl ? fairEl.id : null, helpEl ? helpEl.id : null);

    if (t && trustEl) {
      Plotly.restyle(trustEl, {
        x: [t.x], y: [t.y], text: [t.text],
        hovertext: [t.hovertext], 'marker.color': [t.marker_color]
      }, [0]);
      Plotly.relayout(trustEl, { annotations: t.annotations });
    }
    if (f && fairEl) {
      Plotly.restyle(fairEl, {
        x: [f.x], y: [f.y], text: [f.text],
        hovertext: [f.hovertext], 'marker.color': [f.marker_color]
      }, [0]);
      Plotly.relayout(fairEl, { annotations: f.annotations });
    }
    if (h && helpEl) {
      Plotly.restyle(helpEl, {
        x: [h.x], y: [h.y], text: [h.text],
        hovertext: [h.hovertext], 'marker.color': [h.marker_color]
      }, [0]);
      Plotly.relayout(helpEl, { annotations: h.annotations });
    }
  } catch (err) {
    console.error('ESS country filter update failed:', err);
  }
};

document.addEventListener('change', function (e) {
  if (e.target && e.target.id === 'country-select') {
    console.log('[ess-filter] country-select changed to index', e.target.value);
    window.essUpdateCountry(parseInt(e.target.value, 10));
  }
});

console.log('[ess-filter] country-filter.js loaded OK,', window.essTrustData.length, 'countries available.');
console.log('[ess-filter] plotly divs on page:', Array.prototype.slice.call(document.querySelectorAll('.js-plotly-plot')).map(function (e) { return e.id; }));
"
)
writeLines(filter_js, "country-filter.js")
```

## {.sidebar}

```{r}
#| label: country-filter
#| output: asis
options_html <- paste0(
  '<option value="', seq_along(groups) - 1, '">', group_labels, '</option>',
  collapse = "\n"
)

cat(paste0(
  '<div class="ess-filter">',
  '<label for="country-select">Country</label>',
  '<select id="country-select" class="form-select">',
  options_html,
  '</select>',
  '</div>',
  '<script src="country-filter.js"></script>'
))
```

## Column

### Row

```{r}
#| label: ppltrst-bar
initial <- ppltrst_by_country[[1]]

plot_ly(elementId = "ppltrst-bar") |>
  add_trace(
    data = initial,
    x = ~factor(ppltrst, levels = 0:10),
    y = ~percentage,
    type = "bar",
    marker = list(color = bar_colors_by_country[[1]]),
    text = ~paste0("<b>", percentage_precise, "%</b>"),
    textposition = "outside",
    textfont = list(family = "Inter, sans-serif", size = 16, color = "#1a1a1a"),
    hovertext = ~paste0("<b>", formatC(n, big.mark = " ", format = "d"), "</b> respondents"),
    hoverinfo = "text"
  ) |>
  layout(
    title = list(
      text = paste0(
        "<b>Most People Can Be Trusted</b> ",
        "<span style='color:#666666;font-weight:400;'>(0: You can't be too careful, 10: Most people can be trusted)</span>"
      ),
      font = title_font,
      x = 0
    ),
    annotations = list(avg_annotation(avg_scores[1]), n_annotation(total_n[1])),
    margin = list(t = 100, pad = 8),
    showlegend = FALSE,
    dragmode = FALSE,
    hovermode = "x",
    hoverlabel = list(
      bgcolor = "#6B1B1E",
      bordercolor = "#6B1B1E",
      font = list(color = "#ffffff", family = "Inter, sans-serif", size = 13)
    ),
    xaxis = list(title = list(text = "Trust level (0 - 10)",
                              font = list(family = "Inter, sans-serif", size = 12, color = "#555555")),
                 showgrid = FALSE, showline = FALSE, zeroline = FALSE,
                 type = "category",
                 categoryorder = "array",
                 categoryarray = as.character(0:10),
                 tickmode = "array",
                 tickvals = as.character(0:10),
                 ticktext = as.character(0:10),
                 fixedrange = TRUE,
                 tickfont = list(family = "Inter, sans-serif", size = 12)),
    yaxis = list(title = "", showgrid = FALSE, showline = FALSE,
                 zeroline = FALSE, tickfont = list(family = "Inter, sans-serif", size = 13),
                 ticksuffix = "%",
                 range = c(0, 40),
                 fixedrange = TRUE),
    paper_bgcolor = "white",
    plot_bgcolor = "white"
  ) |>
  config(displayModeBar = FALSE)
```

### Row

```{r}
#| label: pplfair-bar
initial_fair <- pplfair_by_country[[1]]

plot_ly(elementId = "pplfair-bar") |>
  add_trace(
    data = initial_fair,
    x = ~factor(pplfair, levels = 0:10),
    y = ~percentage,
    type = "bar",
    marker = list(color = bar_colors_by_country_fair[[1]]),
    text = ~paste0("<b>", percentage_precise, "%</b>"),
    textposition = "outside",
    textfont = list(family = "Inter, sans-serif", size = 16, color = "#1a1a1a"),
    hovertext = ~paste0("<b>", formatC(n, big.mark = " ", format = "d"), "</b> respondents"),
    hoverinfo = "text"
  ) |>
  layout(
    title = list(
      text = paste0(
        "<b>Most People Try to Be Fair</b> ",
        "<span style='color:#666666;font-weight:400;'>(0: Most people try to take advantage of me, 10: Most people try to be fair)</span>"
      ),
      font = title_font,
      x = 0
    ),
    annotations = list(avg_annotation_fair(avg_scores_fair[1]), n_annotation(total_n_fair[1])),
    margin = list(t = 100, pad = 8),
    showlegend = FALSE,
    dragmode = FALSE,
    hovermode = "x",
    hoverlabel = list(
      bgcolor = "#6B1B1E",
      bordercolor = "#6B1B1E",
      font = list(color = "#ffffff", family = "Inter, sans-serif", size = 13)
    ),
    xaxis = list(title = list(text = "Fairness level (0 - 10)",
                              font = list(family = "Inter, sans-serif", size = 12, color = "#555555")),
                 showgrid = FALSE, showline = FALSE, zeroline = FALSE,
                 type = "category",
                 categoryorder = "array",
                 categoryarray = as.character(0:10),
                 tickmode = "array",
                 tickvals = as.character(0:10),
                 ticktext = as.character(0:10),
                 fixedrange = TRUE,
                 tickfont = list(family = "Inter, sans-serif", size = 12)),
    yaxis = list(title = "", showgrid = FALSE, showline = FALSE,
                 zeroline = FALSE, tickfont = list(family = "Inter, sans-serif", size = 13),
                 ticksuffix = "%",
                 range = c(0, 40),
                 fixedrange = TRUE),
    paper_bgcolor = "white",
    plot_bgcolor = "white"
  ) |>
  config(displayModeBar = FALSE)
```

### Row

```{r}
#| label: pplhlp-bar
initial_help <- pplhlp_by_country[[1]]

plot_ly(elementId = "pplhlp-bar") |>
  add_trace(
    data = initial_help,
    x = ~factor(pplhlp, levels = 0:10),
    y = ~percentage,
    type = "bar",
    marker = list(color = bar_colors_by_country_help[[1]]),
    text = ~paste0("<b>", percentage_precise, "%</b>"),
    textposition = "outside",
    textfont = list(family = "Inter, sans-serif", size = 16, color = "#1a1a1a"),
    hovertext = ~paste0("<b>", formatC(n, big.mark = " ", format = "d"), "</b> respondents"),
    hoverinfo = "text"
  ) |>
  layout(
    title = list(
      text = paste0(
        "<b>Most People Try to Be Helpful</b> ",
        "<span style='color:#666666;font-weight:400;'>(0: People mostly look out for themselves, 10: People mostly try to be helpful)</span>"
      ),
      font = title_font,
      x = 0
    ),
    annotations = list(avg_annotation_help(avg_scores_help[1]), n_annotation(total_n_help[1])),
    margin = list(t = 100, pad = 8),
    showlegend = FALSE,
    dragmode = FALSE,
    hovermode = "x",
    hoverlabel = list(
      bgcolor = "#6B1B1E",
      bordercolor = "#6B1B1E",
      font = list(color = "#ffffff", family = "Inter, sans-serif", size = 13)
    ),
    xaxis = list(title = list(text = "Helpfulness level (0 - 10)",
                              font = list(family = "Inter, sans-serif", size = 12, color = "#555555")),
                 showgrid = FALSE, showline = FALSE, zeroline = FALSE,
                 type = "category",
                 categoryorder = "array",
                 categoryarray = as.character(0:10),
                 tickmode = "array",
                 tickvals = as.character(0:10),
                 ticktext = as.character(0:10),
                 fixedrange = TRUE,
                 tickfont = list(family = "Inter, sans-serif", size = 12)),
    yaxis = list(title = "", showgrid = FALSE, showline = FALSE,
                 zeroline = FALSE, tickfont = list(family = "Inter, sans-serif", size = 13),
                 ticksuffix = "%",
                 range = c(0, 40),
                 fixedrange = TRUE),
    paper_bgcolor = "white",
    plot_bgcolor = "white"
  ) |>
  config(displayModeBar = FALSE)
```
### Row

```{r}
#| label: footer
#| output: asis
cat('<p style="font-size: 12px; color: #555555; font-family: Inter, sans-serif; text-align: right; padding: 0.5rem 1rem;">
Data: <a href="https://ess.sikt.no/en/" target="_blank" style="color: #555555; text-decoration: underline;">The European Social Survey, Round 11 (2023/24)</a>
</p>')
```
